core_behavior: The system must validate incoming HTTP requests to ensure all required fields are present.
If any required field is missing, the system must return an HTTP 400 Bad Request status code.
The system must generate a structured error response that identifies all missing fields.
inputs: An HTTP request containing various fields, some of which are required for processing.
outputs: An HTTP 400 Bad Request response when required fields are missing.
A structured error response in the body of the HTTP 400 response, detailing the missing fields.
The JSON error response will have the following structure: { "error": "Missing required fields", "missingFields": ["field1", "field2", ...] }
The "missingFields" array will contain the names of all required fields that are missing from the request.
assumptions: The structured error response will be in JSON format, as it is a common format for API error responses.
The list of required fields is predefined and known to the system.
The system will only check for the presence of fields, not their validity or format.
The system assumes that the request is well-formed in terms of syntax (e.g., valid JSON if JSON is used).
failure_modes: If the request is malformed (e.g., not valid JSON), the system will not proceed to check for missing fields and will instead return an HTTP 400 with a different error message indicating a malformed request.
If the system encounters an unexpected error while processing the request, it will return an HTTP 500 Internal Server Error with a generic error message.
edge_cases: If all required fields are present, the system will proceed with normal processing and not return an error.
If none of the required fields are present, the system will list all required fields as missing in the error response.
If the request contains additional fields that are not required, the system will ignore these fields and only focus on the presence of required fields.
security_constraints: The system must not expose any sensitive information in the error response.
The system must ensure that the error response does not inadvertently reveal information about the internal structure or logic of the application.
performance_constraints: The system must perform the validation of required fields and generate the error response within a reasonable time frame to ensure a responsive user experience.
The system should be able to handle a high volume of requests without significant degradation in performance.
